Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 824 upvotes, 90 comments
  2. MariaDB innovation: vector index performance 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Sending Jabber/XMPP Messages via HTTP 42 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. The Enterprise Context Layer 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Show HN: RunAnwhere – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 89 upvotes, 23 comments
  6. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 165 upvotes, 51 comments
  7. Show HN: DD Photos – open-source photo album site generator (Go and SvelteKit) 47 upvotes, 11 comments
  8. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 145 upvotes, 48 comments
  9. I used pulsar detection techniques to turn a phone into a watch timegrapher 27 upvotes, 5 comments
  10. PgAdmin 4 9.13 with AI Assistant Panel 70 upvotes, 20 comments
  11. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 231 upvotes, 97 comments
  12. I put my whole life into a single database 345 upvotes, 164 comments
  13. Defeat as Method 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Open Weights Isn't Open Training 15 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. More agent tools and AI tools should be pricing on outcomes (2025) 12 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. Billion-Parameter Theories 21 upvotes, 6 comments
  17. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 351 upvotes, 213 comments
  18. We are building data breach machines and nobody cares 24 upvotes, 8 comments
  19. Levels of Agentic Engineering 13 upvotes, 3 comments
  20. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Meta acquires Moltbook 229 upvotes, 144 comments
  22. Throwing away 18 months of code and starting over 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  23. How many options fit into a boolean? 35 upvotes, 17 comments
  24. Rebasing in Magit 135 upvotes, 95 comments
  25. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 161 upvotes, 126 comments
  26. A New Version of Our Oracle Solaris Environment for Developers 38 upvotes, 24 comments
  27. RFC 454545 – Human Em Dash Standard 79 upvotes, 60 comments
  28. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 298 upvotes, 315 comments
  29. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 36 upvotes, 37 comments
  30. I built a programming language using Claude Code 43 upvotes, 54 comments

Last generated 2:30am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
